setting.note: Ondaatje's Sri Lanka is a country under the pressure of civil war in the late 1980s and early 1990s, seen through a forensic investigation. The map runs from the capital, Colombo, up the central axis to the ancient Buddhist ruins and monasteries of the north-central plain and out to the tea-country hills, where the war's disappearances surface as bones.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Michael Ondaatje, first published 2000. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Anil's Ghost, Michael Ondaatje (2000). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: A fired-earth red for laterite soil, terracotta and the ochre of temple stone. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
